- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Sophie.
- Vu wou kënnt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg.
- A Dir?
- Ech sinn aus Däitschland.
- Wéi al sidd Dir?
- Ech sinn 25 Joer al.
- A wéi laang liewt Dir schonn hei?
- Ech liewen zënter fënnef Joer hei.
- Wat maacht Dir berufflech?
- Ech sinn Léierin.
- A wat maacht Dir gär an der Fräizäit?
- Ech liesen gär a spillen Tennis.
- Dat kléngt flott!
- Jo, an Dir?
- Ech héieren gär Musek a kachen.
- Super!
- Merci.
- Gären.
- What is your name?
- My name is Sophie.
- And where are you from?
- I come from Luxembourg.
- And you?
- I am from Germany.
- How old are you?
- I am 25 years old.
- And how long have you lived here?
- I have lived here for five years.
- What do you do professionally?
- I am a teacher.
- And what do you like to do in your spare time?
- I like reading and playing tennis.
- That sounds great!
- Yes, and you?
- I enjoy listening to music and cooking.
- Great!
- Thank you.
- You’re welcome.

- Moin Marco, wéi geet et?
- Moin Fabienne, gutt. An dir?
- Och ganz gutt. Ech si just op Besuch, ech fuere jo lo an Italien.
- Also, a wou an Italien?
- Am Norden, zu Mailand.
- Zu Mailand? Nun do ast schön.
- Jo, dat ass vuer. Wunnts du nach immer zu Lëtzebuerg?
- Jo, zu Esch. So, bas du nach e puer Deeg hei?
- Jo, nach eng Woch.
- Ma da komm en Käer bei Mösch ob Esch.
- Oh, dat ass eng gutt Idee.
- Sonndisch?
- Jo, gär. Weijast den Adress?
- 1216 Letzebuerger Stroos, L421 Esch-Uelzest.
- Moment, ech muss dat notéieren.
- Hei, ech ginn da min Kärt, dat ass mir einfach.
- Merci.
- Hello Marco, how are you?
- Hello Fabienne, good. And you?
- Also very good. I am just visiting, I am going to Italy soon.
- So, and where in Italy?
- In the north, in Milan.
- In Milan? Well then you’re lovely.
- Yes, that’s true. Do you still come to Luxembourg sometimes?
- Yes, in Esch. So, are you staying here a few more days?
- Yes, another week.
- Then a cheese from Mösch in Esch will come.
- Oh, that’s a good idea.
- Sunday?
- Yes, gladly. Do you remember the address?
- 1216 Letzebuerger Street, L421 Esch-Uelzest.
- Moment, I must note that down.
- Here, I’ll give you my card, that’s easier for me.
- Thanks.

- And you?
- I am a student.

- Bonjour Madame, wéi kann ech Iech hëllefen?
- Gude Moin, ech si geplënnert an ech wollt mech hei an der Stad umellen.
- Wéi ass Äre Numm?
- Sandy Vinandi.
- Äre Gebuertsdatum, wann ech gelift?
- Ech sinn de 15. Januar 1974 gebuer.
- Wéi ass Är nei Adress?
- 20, Rue du Parc, 15, 24, hei an der Stad.
- Wunnt nach eng Persoun op der Adress?
- Jo, mäi Mann, de Carlos Pinto, a meng zwee Kanner.
- Da brauch ech nach de Gebuertsdatum vun äre Kanner.
- Mäi Meedchen Judith ass den 22. Dezember 2009 gebuer.
- A mäi Bëb den Oskar, den 30. August 2011.
- Jo, an am Moment?
- Voila, hei ass et fäerdeg.
- Dir sidd elo offiziell Awunnerin vun der Stad Lëtzebuerg.
- Fir äre Dossier komplett ze maachen, brauch ech och nach eng Telefonsnummer.
- De fixen Telefon oder Handy Nummer?
- Wéi Dir wëllt, Madame.
- Meng Handy Nummer ass 6 41 12 56 32.
- An eng E-Mail Adress?
- SandyWin@ptt.lu
- Gutt, dat ass alles.
- Merci, Ava.
- Ava.
- Hello Madam, how can I help you?
- Good morning, I am planning and I wanted to register me here in the city.
- What is your name?
- Sandy Vinandi.
- Your birth date, please?
- I was born on January 15, 1974.
- What is your new address?
- 20, Rue du Parc, 15, 24, here in the city.
- Is there another person living at the address?
- Yes, my husband, Carlos Pinto, and my two children.
- Then I also need the birth date of your children.
- My girl Judith was born on December 22, 2009.
- And my boy Oskar, on August 30, 2011.
- Yes, and at the moment?
- Here it is, it’s done.
- You are now officially a resident of the city of Luxembourg.
- To complete your file, I also need a phone number.
- The fixed landline or mobile number?
- As you wish, ma’am.
- My mobile number is 6 41 12 56 32.
- And an email address?
- SandyWin@ptt.lu
- Good, that’s all.
- Thank you, Ava.
- Ava.

- Den 23. Juni feiere Lëtzebuerger Nationalfeierdag.
- Den Owend virdrun, also den 22. Juni, ass vill Animatioun an der Stad.
- Nationalfeierdag ass eng grouss Militärparad an der Stad.

- The evening before, so on June 22, there is a lot of entertainment in the city.
- National Day is a large military parade in the city.

- Mammendag a Pappendag.
- Den zweete Sonndeg am Juni ass Mammendag zu Lëtzebuerg,
- an den éischte Sonndeg am Oktober ass Pappendag.
- Kanner baschten den Mammen an de Pappen eppes flottes an der Schoul,
- sangen e Lidd oder presentéieren e Gedicht.
- Deen Dag sinn d'Kanner extra brav.
- Mother’s Day and Father’s Day.
- The second Sunday in June is Mother’s Day in Luxembourg,
- and the first Sunday in October is Father’s Day.
- Children wash the mothers and fathers a little something in school,
- sing a song or present a poem.
- That day the children are extra well-behaved.

- Sprangprozessioun.
- Péngschtsonndeg a Péngschtméindeg si Feierdeeg zu Lëtzebuerg.
- Den Dag dono, also Péngschtdënschdeg, ass zu Iechternach Sprangprozessioun.
- Dat ass e reliéise Cortège.
- D'Leit sprange duerch d'Stroosse vun Iechternach bis bei d'Basilika.
- D'Sprangprozessioun steet zënter November 2010 op der UNESCO-Lëscht.
- Sprangprozession.
- Pentecost Sunday and Pentecost Monday are holidays in Luxembourg.
- The day after, Pentecost Tuesday, there is the Sprangprozession in Echternach.
- It is a religious procession.
- People jump through the streets of Echternach up to the basilica.
- The Sprangprozession has been on the UNESCO list since November 2010.

- Vu Enn August bis Ufank September ass an der Stad um Glacis eng grouss Kiermes.
- Jonk an Al amuséiere sech op den Attraktiounen.
- Si iessen Lëtzebuerger Spezialitéiten, wéi Gromperekichelcher a gebaakene Fësch.
- From August to the beginning of September, there is a large funfair in the city at Glacis.
- The young people and adults enjoy the attractions.
- They eat Luxembourgish specialties, such as potato rösti and baked fish.
